evals.prompt: /forward-briefing — claude-progress.txt has 5 entries, each ending with `Blockers:` lines describing failed attempts. Generate a forward-framed briefing.

files.content:
## 2026-05-10
Worked on auth refactor.
Blockers: SAML lib version mismatch, CI keeps failing on 3.9.

## 2026-05-11
Tried to fix SAML mismatch.
Blockers: still failing, vendor docs are stale.

## 2026-05-12
Reverted to lib v2.
Blockers: token validation now broken in staging.

## 2026-05-13
Rolled forward to v3 again.
Blockers: CI green locally, broken in CI matrix.

## 2026-05-14
Isolated the matrix failure.
Blockers: only fails on Python 3.9; team uses 3.11 locally.

assertions: The response writes `.claude/forward-briefing.md` with three sections per the `structural` step list: Last-session-left-at, Direct-next-steps, Open-questions.
Accumulated `Blockers:` lines are rewritten as `Open-questions` (e.g. 'What pin would the matrix accept?') rather than copied verbatim — the skill's stated reframing per arXiv:2605.08060v1 §4.5.
The output file includes a generation-timestamp header so `surface-progress.sh` can compare freshness, per the SKILL.md `structural` requirement.
claude-progress.txt is NOT modified — the skill produces a derived view, not a replacement, per the 'Append-Only Invariant' section.

evals.prompt: /forward-briefing — claude-progress.txt has 12 entries. Which ones get rewritten?
assertions: The response reads only the last 5 entries from claude-progress.txt, per the `structural` step 'Read last 5 entries' — not the full log.
The response treats blank-line-separated blocks as the entry delimiter, consistent with the structural spec.
The response does not require the user to pre-trim the file — the skill itself slices the window.
Older entries (entries 1-7 of the 12) are not present in the briefing output.

evals.prompt: User says: 'log what we did this session'. Should /forward-briefing run?
assertions: The response declines to run /forward-briefing — the SKILL.md `when_to_use` exclusion explicitly says 'Do NOT use for writing the progress log itself — that's /progress-log.'
The response recommends `/progress-log` as the correct tool for appending session work to claude-progress.txt.
The response also distinguishes from `/session-resume` (full briefing including spec/features/git state), which is a separate sibling per the exclusion clause.
The response does not silently invoke /forward-briefing as a substitute — exclusion clauses are mandatory routing per CLAUDE.md skill conventions.
